text:
Market today: Mood is cautiously constructive. The real driver is a potential hidden Fed catalyst (headline #4) — if Wall Street is under-pricing a dovish pivot, that's rocket fuel for your tech/chip-heavy book. Cybersecurity names are already running (headline #9), which props up CRWD.

Your holdings: AVGO is your biggest problem — down nearly 14%, your largest position, on margin. That's the one that can really move the needle against you. PLTR is down 10.6% and flagged SELL — two losing positions bleeding simultaneously on leverage is dangerous. PTNM is down 15.25% with thin data and a tight stop at $9.50 — watch it closely or cut it. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with limited price history; margin amplifies any gap-down hard.

Watch: (1) Whether the Fed catalyst narrative gains traction — a dovish signal lifts semis and growth names fast. (2) AVGO price action near $336 stop — a break there on margin hurts badly.

Bottom line: Protect AVGO first — it's your largest hole and your margin makes it the most dangerous domino.
